content.content:
===INBOX MODEL===
     271 app/models/inbox.rb
---
# frozen_string_literal: true

# == Schema Information
#
# Table name: inboxes
#
#  id                            :integer          not null, primary key
#  allow_messages_after_resolved :boolean          default(TRUE)
#  auto_assignment_config        :jsonb
#  business_name                 :string
#  channel_type                  :string
#  csat_config                   :jsonb            not null
#  csat_survey_enabled           :boolean          default(FALSE)
#  email_address                 :string
#  enable_auto_assignment        :boolean          default(TRUE)
#  enable_email_collect          :boolean          default(TRUE)
#  greeting_enabled              :boolean          default(FALSE)
#  greeting_message              :string
#  lock_to_single_conversation   :boolean          default(FALSE), not null
#  name                          :string           not null
#  out_of_office_message         :string
#  sender_name_type              :integer          default("friendly"), not null
#  timezone                      :string           default("UTC")
#  working_hours_enabled         :boolean          default(FALSE)
#  created_at                    :datetime         not null
#  updated_at                    :datetime         not null
#  account_id                    :integer          not null
#  channel_id                    :integer          not null
#  portal_id                     :bigint
#
# Indexes
#
#  index_inboxes_on_account_id                   (account_id)
#  index_inboxes_on_channel_id_and_channel_type  (channel_id,channel_type)
#  index_inboxes_on_portal_id                    (portal_id)
#
# Foreign Keys
#
#  fk_rails_...  (portal_id => portals.id)
#

class Inbox < ApplicationRecord
  include Reportable
  include Avatarable
  include OutOfOffisable
  include AccountCacheRevalidator
  include InboxAgentAvailability

  # Not allowing characters:
  validates :name, presence: true
  validates :account_id, presence: true
  validates :timezone, inclusion: { in: TZInfo::Timezone.all_identifiers }
  validates :out_of_office_message, length: { maximum: Limits::OUT_OF_OFFICE_MESSAGE_MAX_LENGTH }
  validates :greeting_message, length: { maximum: Limits::GREETING_MESSAGE_MAX_LENGTH }
  validate :ensure_valid_max_assignment_limit

  belongs_to :account
  belongs_to :portal, optional: true

  belongs_to :channel, polymorphic: true, dependent: :destroy

  has_many :campaigns, dependent: :destroy_async
  has_many :contact_inboxes, dependent: :destroy_async
  has_many :contacts, through: :contact_inboxes

  has_many :inbox_members, dependent: :destroy_async
  has_many :members, through: :inbox_members, source: :user
  has_many :conversations, dependent: :destroy_async
  has_many :messages, dependent: :destroy_async

  has_one :inbox_assignment_policy, dependent: :destroy
  has_one :assignment_policy, through: :inbox_assignment_policy
  has_one :agent_bot_inbox, dependent: :destroy_async
  has_one :agent_bot, through: :agent_bot_inbox
  has_many :webhooks, dependent: :destroy_async
  has_many :hooks, dependent: :destroy_async, class_name: 'Integrations::Hook'

  enum sender_name_type: { friendly: 0, professional: 1 }

  after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents

  after_create_commit :dispatch_create_event
  after_update_commit :dispatch_update_event

  scope :order_by_name, -> { order('lower(name) ASC') }

  # Adds multiple members to the inbox
  # @param user_ids [Array<Integer>] Array of user IDs to add as members
  # @return [void]
  def add_members(user_ids)
    inbox_members.create!(user_ids.map { |user_id| { user_id: user_id } })
    update_account_cache
  end

  # Removes multiple members from the inbox
  # @param user_ids [Array<Integer>] Array of user IDs to remove
  # @return [void]
  def remove_members(user_ids)
    inbox_members.where(user_id: user_ids).destroy_all
    update_account_cache
  end

  # Sanitizes inbox name for balanced email provider compatibility
  # ALLOWS: /'._- and Unicode letters/numbers/emojis
  # REMOVES: Forbidden chars (\<>@"()) + spam-trigger symbols (!#$%&*+=?^`{|}~)
  def sanitized_name
    return default_name_for_blank_name if name.blank?

    sanitized = apply_sanitization_rules(name)
    sanitized.blank? && email? ? display_name_from_email : sanitized
  end

  def sanitized_business_name
    sanitize_raw_name(business_name) || sanitized_name
  end

  def sms?
    channel_type == 'Channel::Sms'
  end

  def facebook?
    channel_type == 'Channel::FacebookPage'
  end

  def instagram?
    (facebook? || instagram_direct?) && channel.instagram_id.present?
  end

  def instagram_direct?
    channel_type == 'Channel::Instagram'
  end

  def tiktok?
    channel_type == 'Channel::Tiktok'
  end

  def web_widget?
    channel_type == 'Channel::WebWidget'
  end

  def api?
    channel_type == 'Channel::Api'
  end

  def email?
    channel_type == 'Channel::Email'
  end

  def twilio?
    channel_type == 'Channel::TwilioSms'
  end

  def twitter?
    channel_type == 'Channel::TwitterProfile'
  end

  def telegram?
    channel_type == 'Channel::Telegram'
  end

  def whatsapp?
    channel_type == 'Channel::Whatsapp'
  end

  def twilio_whatsapp?
    channel_type == 'Channel::TwilioSms' && channel.medium == 'whatsapp'
  end

  def assignable_agents
    (account.users.where(id: members.select(:user_id)) + account.administrators).uniq
  end

  def active_bot?
    agent_bot_inbox&.active? || hooks.where(app_id: %w[dialogflow],
                                            status: 'enabled').count.positive?
  end

  def inbox_type
    channel.name
  end

  def webhook_data
    {
      id: id,
      name: name
    }
  end

  def callback_webhook_url
    case channel_type
    when 'Channel::TwilioSms'
      "#{ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', nil)}/twilio/callback"
    when 'Channel::Sms'
      "#{ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', nil)}/webhooks/sms/#{channel.phone_number.delete_prefix('+')}"
    when 'Channel::Line'
      "#{ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', nil)}/webhooks/line/#{channel.line_channel_id}"
    when 'Channel::Whatsapp'
      "#{ENV.fetch('FRONTEND_URL', nil)}/webhooks/whatsapp/#{channel.phone_number}"
    end
  end

  def member_ids_with_assignment_capacity
    members.ids
  end

  def auto_assignment_v2_enabled?
    account.feature_enabled?('assignment_v2')
  end

  # Callers (Reauthorizable) only invoke this on a real transition, so the previous
  # value is always the inverse of the new boolean value.
  def dispatch_reauthorization_event(reauthorization_required)
    return if ENV['ENABLE_INBOX_EVENTS'].blank?

    changed_attributes = { reauthorization_required: [!reauthorization_required, reauthorization_required] }
    Rails.configuration.dispatcher.dispatch(INBOX_UPDATED, Time.zone.now, inbox: self, changed_attributes: changed_attributes)
  end

  private

  def default_name_for_blank_name
    email? ? display_name_from_email : ''
  end

  def sanitize_raw_name(raw)
    return nil if raw.blank?

    result = apply_sanitization_rules(raw)
    result.presence
  end

  def apply_sanitization_rules(name)
    name.gsub(/[\\<>@"!#$%&*+=?^`{|}~:;()]/, '')        # Remove forbidden chars
        .gsub(/[\x00-\x1F\x7F]/, ' ')                   # Replace control chars with spaces
        .gsub(/\A[[:punct:]]+|[[:punct:]]+\z/, '')      # Remove leading/trailing punctuation
        .gsub(/\s+/, ' ')                               # Normalize spaces
        .strip
  end

  def display_name_from_email
    channel.email.split('@').first.parameterize.titleize
  end

  def dispatch_create_event
    return if ENV['ENABLE_INBOX_EVENTS'].blank?

    Rails.configuration.dispatcher.dispatch(INBOX_CREATED, Time.zone.now, inbox: self)
  end

  def dispatch_update_event
    return if ENV['ENABLE_INBOX_EVENTS'].blank?

    Rails.configuration.dispatcher.dispatch(INBOX_UPDATED, Time.zone.now, inbox: self, changed_attributes: previous_changes)
  end

  def ensure_valid_max_assignment_limit
    # overridden in enterprise/app/models/enterprise/inbox.rb
  end

  def delete_round_robin_agents
    ::AutoAssignment::InboxRoundRobinService.new(inbox: self).clear_queue
  end

  def check_channel_type?
    ['Channel::Email', 'Channel::Api', 'Channel::WebWidget'].include?(channel_type)
  end
end

Inbox.prepend_mod_with('Inbox')
Inbox.include_mod_with('Audit::Inbox')
Inbox.include_mod_with('Concerns::Inbox')

===REAUTHORIZABLE===
# This concern is primarily targeted for business models dependent on external services
# The auth tokens we obtained on their behalf could expire or becomes invalid.
# We would be aware of it until we make the API call to the service and it throws error

# Example:
# when a user changes his/her password, the auth token they provided to chatwoot becomes invalid

# This module helps to capture the errors into a counter and when threshold is passed would mark
# the object to be reauthorized. We will also send an email to the owners alerting them of the error.

# In the UI, we will check for the reauthorization_required? status and prompt the reauthorization flow

module Reauthorizable
  extend ActiveSupport::Concern

  AUTHORIZATION_ERROR_THRESHOLD = 2

  # model attribute
  def reauthorization_required?
    ::Redis::Alfred.get(reauthorization_required_key).present?
  end

  # model attribute
  def authorization_error_count
    ::Redis::Alfred.get(authorization_error_count_key).to_i
  end

  # action to be performed when we receive authorization errors
  # Implement in your exception handling logic for authorization errors
  def authorization_error!
    ::Redis::Alfred.incr(authorization_error_count_key)
    # we are giving precendence to the authorization error threshhold defined in the class
    # so that channels can override the default value
    prompt_reauthorization! if authorization_error_count >= self.class::AUTHORIZATION_ERROR_THRESHOLD
  end

  # Performed automatically if error threshold is breached
  # could used to manually prompt reauthorization if auth scope changes
  def prompt_reauthorization!
    state_changed = !reauthorization_required?

    ::Redis::Alfred.set(reauthorization_required_key, true)

    reauthorization_handlers[self.class.name]&.call(self)

    invalidate_inbox_cache unless instance_of?(::AutomationRule)
    dispatch_inbox_reauthorization_event(true) if state_changed
  end

  def process_integration_hook_reauthorization_emails
    if slack?
      AdministratorNotifications::IntegrationsNotificationMailer.with(account: account).slack_disconnect.deliver_later
    elsif dialogflow?
      AdministratorNotifications::IntegrationsNotificationMailer.with(account: account).dialogflow_disconnect.deliver_later
    end
  end

  def send_channel_reauthorization_email(disconnect_type)
    AdministratorNotifications::ChannelNotificationsMailer.with(account: account).public_send(disconnect_type, inbox).deliver_later
  end

  def handle_automation_rule_reauthorization
    update!(active: false)
    AdministratorNotifications::AccountNotificationMailer.with(account: account).automation_rule_disabled(self).deliver_later
  end

  # call this after you successfully Reauthorized the object in UI
  def reauthorized!
    state_changed = reauthorization_required?

    ::Redis::Alfred.delete(authorization_error_count_key)
    ::Redis::Alfred.delete(reauthorization_required_key)

    invalidate_inbox_cache unless instance_of?(::AutomationRule)
    dispatch_inbox_reauthorization_event(false) if state_changed
  end

  private

  def dispatch_inbox_reauthorization_event(reauthorization_required)
    return unless respond_to?(:inbox)
    return if inbox.blank?

    inbox.dispatch_reauthorization_event(reauthorization_required)
  end

  def reauthorization_handlers
    {
      'Integrations::Hook' => ->(obj) { obj.process_integration_hook_reauthorization_emails },
      'Channel::FacebookPage' => ->(obj) { obj.send_channel_reauthorization_email(:facebook_disconnect) },
      'Channel::Instagram' => ->(obj) { obj.send_channel_reauthorization_email(:instagram_disconnect) },
      'Channel::Tiktok' => ->(obj) { obj.send_channel_reauthorization_email(:tiktok_disconnect) },
      'Channel::Whatsapp' => ->(obj) { obj.send_channel_reauthorization_email(:whatsapp_disconnect) },
      'Channel::Email' => ->(obj) { obj.send_channel_reauthorization_email(:email_disconnect) },
      'AutomationRule' => ->(obj) { obj.handle_automation_rule_reauthorization }
    }
  end

  def invalidate_inbox_cache
    inbox.update_account_cache if inbox.present?
  end

  def authorization_error_count_key
    format(::Redis::Alfred::AUTHORIZATION_ERROR_COUNT, obj_type: self.class.table_name.singularize, obj_id: id)
  end

  def reauthorization_required_key
    format(::Redis::Alfred::REAUTHORIZATION_REQUIRED, obj_type: self.class.table_name.singularize, obj_id: id)
  end
end

module Enterprise::Inbox
  def member_ids_with_assignment_capacity
    return super unless enable_auto_assignment?
    return filter_by_capacity(available_agents).map(&:user_id) if auto_assignment_v2_enabled?

    max_assignment_limit = auto_assignment_config['max_assignment_limit']
    overloaded_agent_ids = max_assignment_limit.present? ? get_agent_ids_over_assignment_limit(max_assignment_limit) : []
    super - overloaded_agent_ids
  end

  def active_bot?
    super || captain_active?
  end

  def captain_active?
    captain_assistant.present? && more_responses?
  end

  private

  def more_responses?
    account.usage_limits[:captain][:responses][:current_available].positive?
  end

  def get_agent_ids_over_assignment_limit(limit)
    conversations
      .open
      .where(account_id: account_id)
      .select(:assignee_id)
      .group(:assignee_id)
      .having("count(*) >= #{limit.to_i}")
      .filter_map(&:assignee_id)
  end

  def ensure_valid_max_assignment_limit
    return if auto_assignment_config['max_assignment_limit'].blank?
    return if auto_assignment_config['max_assignment_limit'].to_i.positive?

    errors.add(:auto_assignment_config, 'max_assignment_limit must be greater than 0')
  end
end

module Enterprise::InboxAgentAvailability
  extend ActiveSupport::Concern

  def member_ids_with_assignment_capacity
    return member_ids unless capacity_filtering_enabled?

    # Get online agents with capacity
    agents = available_agents
    agents = filter_by_capacity(agents)
    agents.map(&:user_id)
  end

  private

  def filter_by_capacity(inbox_members_scope)
    return inbox_members_scope unless capacity_filtering_enabled?

    inbox_members_scope.select do |inbox_member|
      capacity_service.agent_has_capacity?(inbox_member.user, self)
    end
  end

  def capacity_filtering_enabled?
    account.feature_enabled?('advanced_assignment') &&
      account.account_users.joins(:agent_capacity_policy).exists?
  end

  def capacity_service
    @capacity_service ||= Enterprise::AutoAssignment::CapacityService.new
  end
end

app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb
class DeleteObjectJob < ApplicationJob
  queue_as :low

  BATCH_SIZE = 5_000

  def perform(object, user = nil, ip = nil)
    # Pre-purge heavy associations for large objects to avoid
    # timeouts & race conditions due to destroy_async fan-out.
    purge_heavy_associations(object)
    object.destroy!
    process_post_deletion_tasks(object, user, ip)
  end

  def process_post_deletion_tasks(object, user, ip); end

  private

  def heavy_associations
    {
      Account => %i[conversations contacts inboxes reporting_events],
      Inbox => %i[conversations contact_inboxes reporting_events]
    }.freeze
  end

  def purge_heavy_associations(object)
    klass = heavy_associations.keys.find { |k| object.is_a?(k) }
    return unless klass

    heavy_associations[klass].each do |assoc|
      next unless object.respond_to?(assoc)

      batch_destroy(object.public_send(assoc))
    end
  end

  def batch_destroy(relation)
    relation.find_in_batches(batch_size: BATCH_SIZE) do |batch|
      batch.each(&:destroy!)
    end
  end
end

app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb
class DeleteObjectJob < ApplicationJob
  queue_as :low

  BATCH_SIZE = 5_000

  def perform(object, user = nil, ip = nil)
    # Pre-purge heavy associations for large objects to avoid
    # timeouts & race conditions due to destroy_async fan-out.
    purge_heavy_associations(object)
    object.destroy!
    process_post_deletion_tasks(object, user, ip)
  end

  def process_post_deletion_tasks(object, user, ip); end

  private

  def heavy_associations
    {
      Account => %i[conversations contacts inboxes reporting_events],
      Inbox => %i[conversations contact_inboxes reporting_events]
    }.freeze
  end

  def purge_heavy_associations(object)
    klass = heavy_associations.keys.find { |k| object.is_a?(k) }
    return unless klass

    heavy_associations[klass].each do |assoc|
      next unless object.respond_to?(assoc)

      batch_destroy(object.public_send(assoc))
    end
  end

  def batch_destroy(relation)
    relation.find_in_batches(batch_size: BATCH_SIZE) do |batch|
      batch.each(&:destroy!)
    end
  end
end

class Api::V1::Accounts::InboxesController < Api::V1::Accounts::BaseController
  include Api::V1::InboxesHelper
  before_action :fetch_inbox, except: [:index, :create]
  before_action :fetch_agent_bot, only: [:set_agent_bot]
  before_action :validate_limit, only: [:create]
  # we are already handling the authorization in fetch inbox
  before_action :check_authorization, except: [:show]

  include Api::V1::Accounts::Concerns::WhatsappHealthManagement

  def index
    @inboxes = policy_scope(Current.account.inboxes)
               .includes(:channel, :portal, :working_hours, { avatar_attachment: :blob })
               .order_by_name
  end

  def show; end

  # Deprecated: This API will be removed in 2.7.0
  def assignable_agents
    @assignable_agents = @inbox.assignable_agents
  end

  def campaigns
    @campaigns = @inbox.campaigns
  end

  def avatar
    @inbox.avatar.attachment.destroy! if @inbox.avatar.attached?
    head :ok
  end

  def create
    ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
      channel = create_channel
      @inbox = Current.account.inboxes.build(
        {
          name: inbox_name(channel),
          channel: channel
        }.merge(
          permitted_params.except(:channel)
        )
      )
      @inbox.save!
    end
  end

  def update
    inbox_params = permitted_params.except(:channel, :csat_config)
    inbox_params[:csat_config] = format_csat_config(permitted_params[:csat_config]) if permitted_params[:csat_config].present?
    @inbox.update!(inbox_params)
    update_inbox_working_hours
    update_channel if channel_update_required?
  end

  def agent_bot
    @agent_bot = @inbox.agent_bot
  end

  def set_agent_bot
    if @agent_bot
      agent_bot_inbox = @inbox.agent_bot_inbox || AgentBotInbox.new(inbox: @inbox)
      agent_bot_inbox.agent_bot = @agent_bot
      agent_bot_inbox.save!
    elsif @inbox.agent_bot_inbox.present?
      @inbox.agent_bot_inbox.destroy!
    end
    head :ok
  end

  def reset_secret
    return head :not_found unless @inbox.api?

    @inbox.channel.reset_secret!
  end

  def destroy
    ::DeleteObjectJob.perform_later(@inbox, Current.user, request.ip) if @inbox.present?
    render status: :ok, json: { message: I18n.t('messages.inbox_deletetion_response') }
  end

  private

  def fetch_inbox
    @inbox = Current.account.inboxes.find(params[:id])
    authorize @inbox, :show?
  end

  def fetch_agent_bot
    @agent_bot = AgentBot.accessible_to(Current.account).find(params[:agent_bot]) if params[:agent_bot]
  end

  def create_channel
    return unless allowed_channel_types.include?(permitted_params[:channel][:type])

    account_channels_method.create!(permitted_params(channel_type_from_params::EDITABLE_ATTRS)[:channel].except(:type))
  end

  def allowed_channel_types
    %w[web_widget api email line telegram whatsapp sms]
  end

  def update_inbox_working_hours
    @inbox.update_working_hours(params.permit(working_hours: Inbox::OFFISABLE_ATTRS)[:working_hours]) if params[:working_hours]
  end

  def update_channel
    channel_attributes = get_channel_attributes(@inbox.channel_type)
    return if permitted_params(channel_attributes)[:channel].blank?

    validate_and_update_email_channel(channel_attributes) if @inbox.inbox_type == 'Email'

    reauthorize_and_update_channel(channel_attributes)
    update_channel_feature_flags
  end

  def channel_update_required?
    permitted_params(get_channel_attributes(@inbox.channel_type))[:channel].present?
  end

  def validate_and_update_email_channel(channel_attributes)
    validate_email_channel(channel_attributes)
  rescue StandardError => e
    render json: { message: e }, status: :unprocessable_entity and return
  end

  def reauthorize_and_update_channel(channel_attributes)
    @inbox.channel.reauthorized! if @inbox.channel.respond_to?(:reauthorized!)
    @inbox.channel.update!(permitted_params(channel_attributes)[:channel])
  end

  def update_channel_feature_flags
    return unless @inbox.web_widget?
    return unless permitted_params(Channel::WebWidget::EDITABLE_ATTRS)[:channel].key? :selected_feature_flags

    @inbox.channel.selected_feature_flags = permitted_params(Channel::WebWidget::EDITABLE_ATTRS)[:channel][:selected_feature_flags]
    @inbox.channel.save!
  end

  def format_csat_config(config)
    formatted = {
      'display_type' => config['display_type'] || 'emoji',
      'message' => config['message'] || '',
      :survey_rules => {
        'operator' => config.dig('survey_rules', 'operator') || 'contains',
        'values' => config.dig('survey_rules', 'values') || []
      },
      'button_text' => config['button_text'] || 'Please rate us',
      'language' => config['language'] || 'en'
    }
    format_template_config(config, formatted)
    formatted
  end

  def format_template_config(config, formatted)
    formatted['template'] = config['template'] if config['template'].present?
  end

  def inbox_attributes
    [:name, :avatar, :greeting_enabled, :greeting_message, :enable_email_collect, :csat_survey_enabled,
     :enable_auto_assignment, :working_hours_enabled, :out_of_office_message, :timezone, :allow_messages_after_resolved,
     :lock_to_single_conversation, :portal_id, :sender_name_type, :business_name,
     { csat_config: [:display_type, :message, :button_text, :language,
                     { survey_rules: [:operator, { values: [] }],
                       template: [:name, :template_id, :friendly_name, :content_sid, :approval_sid, :created_at, :language, :status] }] }]
  end

  def permitted_params(channel_attributes = [])
    # We will remove this line after fixing https://linear.app/chatwoot/issue/CW-1567/null-value-passed-as-null-string-to-backend
    params.each { |k, v| params[k] = params[k] == 'null' ? nil : v }
    params.permit(*inbox_attributes, channel: [:type, *channel_attributes])
  end

  def channel_type_from_params
    {
      'web_widget' => Channel::WebWidget,
      'api' => Channel::Api,
      'email' => Channel::Email,
      'line' => Channel::Line,
      'telegram' => Channel::Telegram,
      'whatsapp' => Channel::Whatsapp,
      'sms' => Channel::Sms
    }[permitted_params[:channel][:type]]
  end

  def get_channel_attributes(channel_type)
    channel_type.constantize.const_defined?(:EDITABLE_ATTRS) ? channel_type.constantize::EDITABLE_ATTRS.presence : []
  end
end

class AutoAssignment::InboxRoundRobinService
  pattr_initialize [:inbox!]

  # called on inbox delete
  def clear_queue
    ::Redis::Alfred.delete(round_robin_key)
  end

  # called on inbox member create
  def add_agent_to_queue(user_id)
    ::Redis::Alfred.lpush(round_robin_key, user_id)
  end

  # called on inbox member delete
  def remove_agent_from_queue(user_id)
    ::Redis::Alfred.lrem(round_robin_key, user_id)
  end

  def reset_queue
    clear_queue
    add_agent_to_queue(inbox.inbox_members.map(&:user_id))
  end

  # end of queue management functions

  # allowed member ids = [assignable online agents supplied by the assignment service]
  # the values of allowed member ids should be in string format
  def available_agent(allowed_agent_ids: [])
    reset_queue unless validate_queue?
    user_id = get_member_from_allowed_agent_ids(allowed_agent_ids)

class AutoAssignment::InboxRoundRobinService
  pattr_initialize [:inbox!]

  # called on inbox delete
  def clear_queue
    ::Redis::Alfred.delete(round_robin_key)
  end

  # called on inbox member create
  def add_agent_to_queue(user_id)
    ::Redis::Alfred.lpush(round_robin_key, user_id)
  end

  # called on inbox member delete
  def remove_agent_from_queue(user_id)
    ::Redis::Alfred.lrem(round_robin_key, user_id)
  end

  def reset_queue
    clear_queue
    add_agent_to_queue(inbox.inbox_members.map(&:user_id))
  end

  # end of queue management functions

  # allowed member ids = [assignable online agents supplied by the assignment service]
  # the values of allowed member ids should be in string format
  def available_agent(allowed_agent_ids: [])
    reset_queue unless validate_queue?
    user_id = get_member_from_allowed_agent_ids(allowed_agent_ids)
    inbox.inbox_members.find_by(user_id: user_id)&.user if user_id.present?
  end

     1	class AutoAssignment::AssignmentJob < ApplicationJob
     2	  queue_as :default
     3	
     4	  IN_FLIGHT_TTL = 5.minutes
     5	
     6	  # Coalesce per inbox: at most one AssignmentJob per inbox is in-flight
     7	  # (queued or running) at any time. The marker carries a token so a job only
     8	  # releases its own claim (a newer job may have taken it after a TTL lapse).
     9	  def self.enqueue_for_inbox(inbox_id)
    10	    key = format(::Redis::Alfred::AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_IN_FLIGHT_KEY, inbox_id: inbox_id)
    11	    token = SecureRandom.uuid
    12	    return false unless ::Redis::Alfred.set(key, token, nx: true, ex: IN_FLIGHT_TTL)
    13	
    14	    return true if perform_later(inbox_id: inbox_id, token: token)
    15	
    16	    # Enqueue was halted; release our own claim so the inbox isn't gated until the TTL.
    17	    ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
    18	    false
    19	  rescue StandardError
    20	    # Enqueue raised after we claimed the gate; release our own claim, then re-raise.
    21	    ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
    22	    raise
    23	  end
    24	
    25	  def perform(inbox_id:, token: nil)
    26	    inbox = Inbox.find_by(id: inbox_id)
    27	    return unless inbox
    28	
    29	    service = AutoAssignment::AssignmentService.new(inbox: inbox)
    30	    assigned_count = service.perform_bulk_assignment(limit: bulk_assignment_limit)
    31	    Rails.logger.info "Assigned #{assigned_count} conversations for inbox #{inbox.id}"
    32	  rescue StandardError => e
    33	    Rails.logger.error "Bulk assignment failed for inbox #{inbox_id}: #{e.message}"
    34	    raise e if Rails.env.test?
    35	  ensure
    36	    release_in_flight(inbox_id, token)
    37	  end
    38	
    39	  private
    40	
    41	  # Release the in-flight marker only if we still own it. The atomic
    42	  # compare-and-delete ensures a job whose TTL lapsed can't delete a newer
    43	  # job's claim. Tokenless (pre-deploy) jobs never claimed a key, so skip.
    44	  def release_in_flight(inbox_id, token)
    45	    return if token.nil?
    46	
    47	    key = format(::Redis::Alfred::AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_IN_FLIGHT_KEY, inbox_id: inbox_id)
    48	    ::Redis::Alfred.delete_if_equals(key, token)
    49	  end
    50	
    51	  def bulk_assignment_limit
    52	    ENV.fetch('AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_BULK_LIMIT', 100).to_i
    53	  end
    54	end

     1	require 'net/imap'
     2	
     3	class Inboxes::FetchImapEmailsJob < MutexApplicationJob
     4	  queue_as :scheduled_jobs
     5	
     6	  def perform(channel, interval = 1)
     7	    return unless should_fetch_email?(channel)
     8	
     9	    key = format(::Redis::Alfred::EMAIL_MESSAGE_MUTEX, inbox_id: channel.inbox.id)
    10	
    11	    with_lock(key, 5.minutes) do
    12	      process_email_for_channel(channel, interval)
    13	    end
    14	  rescue *ExceptionList::IMAP_EXCEPTIONS => e
    15	    Rails.logger.error "Authorization error for email channel - #{channel.inbox.id} : #{e.message}"
    16	  rescue IOError, OpenSSL::SSL::SSLError, Net::IMAP::NoResponseError, Net::IMAP::BadResponseError, Net::IMAP::InvalidResponseError,
    17	         Net::IMAP::ResponseParseError, Net::IMAP::ResponseReadError, Net::IMAP::ResponseTooLargeError => e
    18	    Rails.logger.error "Error for email channel - #{channel.inbox.id} : #{e.message}"
    19	  rescue LockAcquisitionError
    20	    Rails.logger.error "Lock failed for #{channel.inbox.id}"
    21	  rescue StandardError => e
    22	    ChatwootExceptionTracker.new(e, account: channel.account).capture_exception
    23	  end
    24	
    25	  private
    26	
    27	  def should_fetch_email?(channel)
    28	    channel.imap_enabled? && !channel.reauthorization_required?
    29	  end
    30	
    31	  def process_email_for_channel(channel, interval)
    32	    inbound_emails = if channel.microsoft?
    33	                       Imap::MicrosoftFetchEmailService.new(channel: channel, interval: interval).perform
    34	                     elsif channel.google?
    35	                       Imap::GoogleFetchEmailService.new(channel: channel, interval: interval).perform
    36	                     else
    37	                       Imap::FetchEmailService.new(channel: channel, interval: interval).perform
    38	                     end
    39	
    40	    inbound_emails.each do |inbound_mail|
    41	      process_mail(inbound_mail, channel)
    42	    end
    43	  rescue OAuth2::Error => e
    44	    Rails.logger.error "Error for email channel - #{channel.inbox.id} : #{e.message}"
    45	    channel.authorization_error!
    46	  end
    47	
    48	  def should_skip_email?(message_id)
    49	    failure_count = Rails.cache.read("email_failures:#{message_id}") || 0
    50	    failure_count >= 3
    51	  end
    52	
    53	  def mark_email_as_failed(message_id)
    54	    failure_count = Rails.cache.read("email_failures:#{message_id}") || 0
    55	    Rails.cache.write("email_failures:#{message_id}", failure_count + 1, expires_in: 6.hours)
    56	  end
    57	
    58	  def process_mail(inbound_mail, channel)
    59	    # Skip if this email has failed multiple times recently
    60	    if should_skip_email?(inbound_mail.message_id)
    61	      Rails.logger.warn "[IMAP] Skipping problematic email: #{inbound_mail.message_id}"
    62	      return
    63	    end
    64	
    65	    begin
    66	      Timeout.timeout(email_processing_timeout) do
    67	        Imap::ImapMailbox.new.process(inbound_mail, channel)
    68	      end
    69	    rescue Timeout::Error
    70	      mark_email_as_failed(inbound_mail.message_id)
    71	      Rails.logger.error "[IMAP] Email processing timeout (#{email_processing_timeout}s): #{inbound_mail.message_id}"
    72	    rescue StandardError => e
    73	      mark_email_as_failed(inbound_mail.message_id)
    74	      Rails.logger.error "[IMAP] Failed to process email #{inbound_mail.message_id}: #{e.message}"
    75	      ChatwootExceptionTracker.new(e, account: channel.account).capture_exception
    76	    end
    77	  end
    78	
    79	  def email_processing_timeout
    80	    GlobalConfigService.load('EMAIL_PROCESSING_TIMEOUT_SECONDS', 60).to_i
    81	  end
    82	end

content.text:
I now have a complete picture. Here is the full audit.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project.** Chatwoot — a Rails (backend) + Vue 3 (frontend) open-source customer-engagement / omnichannel support platform. An **Account** is the tenant; an **Inbox** is a communication channel (web widget, email, WhatsApp, SMS, etc.); **Conversations** and **Contacts** flow through inboxes.

**Layout (Rails-conventional, plus an Enterprise overlay):**
- `app/models/` — domain models; `app/controllers/`, `app/policies/`, `app/builders/`, `app/finders/`, `app/services/`, `app/jobs/`, `app/listeners/`, `app/views/` (jbuilder).
- `lib/` — integrations (`lib/integrations/...`), seeders, rake tasks, Redis keys, event types.
- `enterprise/` — a paid-tier overlay that **prepends/includes modules into OSS classes** via `prepend_mod_with` / `include_mod_with` (e.g. `Inbox.prepend_mod_with('Inbox')` at `app/models/inbox.rb:269-271`). Always check `enterprise/` for a mirror when touching core.
- `app/javascript/` — Vue 3 frontend; `spec/` — RSpec; `db/` — schema/migrations.

## Step 3 — Teardown trace (inbox + attached data)

Order of execution when an inbox is destroyed:

1. **Trigger (request layer):** `Api::V1::Accounts::InboxesController#destroy` (`app/controllers/api/v1/accounts/inboxes_controller.rb:77-80`) → enqueues `DeleteObjectJob.perform_later(@inbox, Current.user, request.ip)`. (Account-level teardown enters the same job: `app/controllers/super_admin/accounts_controller.rb:60-63` → `DeleteObjectJob.perform_later(account)`.)

2. **Job — pre-purge heavy associations (inline, batched):** `DeleteObjectJob#perform` (`app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb`) calls `purge_heavy_associations` **before** `object.destroy!`. For an `Inbox`, `heavy_associations` (`:18-22`) = `%i[conversations contact_inboxes reporting_events]`; `batch_destroy` (`:36-40`) walks them in batches of 5,000 calling `destroy!` on each record **synchronously**, to avoid `destroy_async` fan-out timeouts/races. (For an `Account` it's `conversations contacts inboxes reporting_events`; the Enterprise overlay `enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/delete_object_job.rb` also adds `SlaPolicy => [:applied_slas]`.)

3. **`object.destroy!`** (`app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb`, after purge) fires the model `dependent:` chain on `app/models/inbox.rb`:
   - **Inline (`:destroy`):** `channel` (`:60`, the `Channel::*` row + its own `has_one :inbox dependent: :destroy_async`), `inbox_assignment_policy` (`:71`), `reporting_events` (Reportable, `reportable.rb:7`), and Enterprise `captain_inbox` + `inbox_capacity_limits`.
   - **Async (`:destroy_async`, enqueues `ActiveRecord::DestroyAssociationAsyncJob`):** `campaigns`, `contact_inboxes` (→ each `has_many :conversations dependent: :destroy_async`, `app/models/contact_inbox.rb:34`), `inbox_members`, `conversations` (→ `messages`, `mentions`, `csat_survey_response`, `conversation_participants`, `notifications`, `reporting_events` — `app/models/conversation.rb:109-115`), `messages`, `webhooks`, `hooks`, `agent_bot_inbox`, `working_hours` (OutOfOffisable), plus Enterprise `calls`.
   - Note conversations/contact_inboxes are *already* purged inline in step 2, so the async pass typically finds them gone — the async declaration is the fallback path for the non-batched (direct `inbox.destroy!`) route.

4. **`after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents`** (`app/models/inbox.rb:80,260`) → `AutoAssignment::InboxRoundRobinService#clear_queue` (`app/services/auto_assignment/inbox_round_robin_service.rb:5-7`) deletes the inbox's Redis round-robin key. `after_commit :update_account_cache` (AccountCacheRevalidator) also runs.

5. **Post-deletion (Enterprise only):** `DeleteObjectJob#process_post_deletion_tasks` → `enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/delete_object_job.rb#create_audit_entry` writes an `Enterprise::AuditLog` (`action: 'destroy'`) for `Inbox`/`Conversation`/`SlaPolicy` when a `user` is present.

**Async vs inline summary:** *Inline* = channel, inbox_assignment_policy, reporting_events, (EE) captain_inbox, inbox_capacity_limits, **and the batched purge of conversations/contact_inboxes/reporting_events in the job**. *Async* = campaigns, inbox_members, agent_bot_inbox, webhooks, hooks, working_hours, (EE) calls, and the declarative conversation/contact_inbox/message paths.

## Step 5 — How inbox availability & identity are guarded in background work

**Assignment (per-inbox bulk):**
- `AutoAssignment::PeriodicAssignmentJob#perform` (`app/jobs/auto_assignment/periodic_assignment_job.rb:4-18`) — re-loads inboxes fresh each run via `account.inboxes.joins(:assignment_policy).find_in_batches` (`:9`); double-guards with `account.feature_enabled?('assignment_v2')` (`:7`) and `inbox.auto_assignment_v2_enabled?` (`:11`, → `app/models/inbox.rb:206`). It passes only `inbox.id` forward (`:13`), never a stale object.
- `AutoAssignment::AssignmentJob.enqueue_for_inbox` (`app/jobs/auto_assignment/assignment_job.rb:9-23`) — Redis in-flight lock keyed by `inbox_id` (`AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_IN_FLIGHT_KEY`) so at most one job per inbox; token-based compare-and-delete release (`:44-49`).
- `AutoAssignment::AssignmentJob#perform` (`:25-37`) — **identity guard:** `inbox = Inbox.find_by(id: inbox_id); return unless inbox` (`:26-27`) — re-fetches by id and bails if the inbox was destroyed between enqueue and run.
- `AutoAssignment::AssignmentService` (`app/services/auto_assignment/assignment_service.rb`) — `return 0 unless inbox.auto_assignment_v2_enabled?` (`:5`), `return 0 unless inbox.enable_auto_assignment?` (`:6`), `return 0 if inbox.available_agents.empty?` (`:10`); row-locks conversations (`:94`) and `conversation.reload` (`:79`).
- Round-robin: `AutoAssignment::InboxRoundRobinService#validate_queue?` (`inbox_round_robin_service.rb:51-53`) reconciles the Redis queue against `inbox.inbox_members` before serving, `reset_queue` (`:23`) on mismatch; `AgentAssignmentService` re-derives the service from `conversation.inbox` (`agent_assignment_service.rb:32`).

**Fetching (IMAP/email):**
- `Inboxes::FetchImapEmailInboxesJob#should_fetch_emails?` (`app/jobs/inboxes/fetch_imap_email_inboxes_job.rb:14-22`) — guards `inbox.account.suspended?`, `inbox.channel.imap_enabled`, `inbox.channel.reauthorization_required?`, plus cloud/plan checks; iterates a fresh `Inbox.where(channel_type:'Channel::Email').find_each` (`:6-7`).
- `Inboxes::FetchImapEmailsJob` (`fetch_imap_emails_job.rb`) — operates on the **channel**, re-checks `should_fetch_email?` = `imap_enabled? && !reauthorization_required?` (`:27-29`); `MutexApplicationJob` lock keyed by `channel.inbox.id` (`:9-13`); `channel.authorization_error!` on OAuth failure (`:45`, drives Reauthorizable threshold).

**Periodic maintenance / orphans:**
- `Internal::RemoveStaleContactInboxesService` (`remove_stale_contact_inboxes_service.rb`) — env-flag gated (`:24-28`); selects via `ContactInbox.stale_without_conversations` (`contact_inbox.rb:37`) and batched `delete_all` (`:14-19`).
- `Internal::RemoveOrphanConversationsService` (`remove_orphan_conversations_service.rb:26-32`) — **the explicit teardown-safety net:** `left_outer_joins(:contact, :inbox)` then `where(inboxes: { id: nil })` finds conversations whose inbox row is gone (a direct consequence of async teardown leaving orphans) and `destroy_all`s them; invoked from `lib/tasks/ops/cleanup_orphan_conversations.rake`.

**Pattern:** background code never trusts a passed-in inbox object — it either passes `inbox_id` and re-`find`s (`return unless inbox`), re-queries from the account/`Inbox` scope, or guards on `account.suspended?` / feature flags / `reauthorization_required?` before acting.

## Step 6 — Blast radius of the contract change (ranked by risk)

The change touches **teardown** and **a couple of associations'** behavior. Risk ranking:

**🔴 Critical — most likely to break / page someone:**
1. `app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb` (+ `enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/delete_object_job.rb`) — the `heavy_associations` map (`:18-22`) hard-codes `Inbox => [conversations, contact_inboxes, reporting_events]`. **If you rename/repurpose any of those associations or change destroy semantics, the batched purge silently no-ops** (it guards `next unless object.respond_to?(assoc)`), leaving heavy data behind → orphan storms. Highest blast radius.
2. The `dependent:` declarations on `app/models/inbox.rb:60-76` — changing inline↔async on `channel` (`:60`), `inbox_assignment_policy` (`:71`), `reporting_events` (Reportable `:7`), or any async member alters what's torn down synchronously. Async fan-out (`ActiveRecord::DestroyAssociationAsyncJob`) is what the job exists to avoid; regressing it reintroduces destroy timeouts.
3. `Internal::RemoveOrphanConversationsService` / `cleanup_orphan_conversations.rake` (`:26-32`) — depends on `conversations.belongs_to :inbox` being nullable-after-teardown. If teardown order or FK behavior changes, this query's `inboxes: { id: nil }` semantics shift.
4. Enterprise teardown overlay: `enterprise/app/models/enterprise/concerns/inbox.rb` (`captain_inbox dependent: :destroy`, `inbox_capacity_limits dependent: :destroy`, `calls dependent: :destroy_async`) and `enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/delete_object_job.rb` audit log — must be re-verified in lockstep; OSS-only testing will miss these.

**🟠 High — strong coupling to teardown/associations:**
5. `app/models/concerns/channelable.rb:6` — the reverse `has_one :inbox, dependent: :destroy_async, touch: true`. Channel-side teardown (account destroy hits `Channel::*` directly via `account.rb:78-100`) and inbox-side `belongs_to :channel dependent: :destroy` form a **two-way destroy** — a change here risks destroy loops or double-destroy.
6. `app/models/account.rb:83` `has_many :inboxes, dependent: :destroy_async` + the account path through the same `DeleteObjectJob` (`heavy_associations[Account]` includes `inboxes`). Account teardown is the larger-scale exercise of the same contract.
7. `app/models/contact_inbox.rb:34` (`has_many :conversations, dependent: :destroy_async`) and `:37` stale scope — sit between inbox and conversations in the destroy chain.
8. `after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents` (`inbox.rb:80,260`) + `inbox_member.rb:25-26` round-robin Redis cleanup — Redis side-effects on destroy.

**🟡 Medium — read/validate the inbox, break only if identity/availability assumptions change:**
9. Assignment pipeline: `assignment_job.rb:26-27` (`find_by` guard), `assignment_service.rb`, `periodic_assignment_job.rb`, round-robin services — already defensively guarded (Step 5) but exercise the inbox heavily.
10. IMAP fetch jobs (`fetch_imap_email*.rb`) — operate on `channel`/`channel.inbox`; if the channel↔inbox link timing changes during teardown, `channel.inbox.id` references (`:9,15,18`) could NPE.
11. `Integrations::Hook` (`hook.rb:40`, `attr_readonly :inbox_id`), `webhook.rb:23` (optional), `reporting_event.rb:37` (optional), `working_hour.rb:24`, `campaign.rb:45` — FK holders; verify nullify-vs-destroy expectations.

**🟢 Lower — mostly read-path, validate-after:**
12. Reports builders (`app/builders/v2/reports/*`), finders (`conversation_finder.rb`), filter/search services, provider send/incoming services — consume inbox attributes/predicates; break only if the public method surface (Step 2) changes, not the teardown.

**Must re-verify after the change:** the full `DeleteObjectJob` path (inbox destroy + account destroy) in both OSS and Enterprise; no orphaned `conversations`/`contact_inboxes`/`messages`/`reporting_events`/`calls`/`applied_slas`; Redis cleanup (round-robin key, in-flight key, IMAP mutex); Enterprise audit-log creation; assignment job's `return unless inbox` guard still fires; orphan-cleanup service query still matches.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project.** Chatwoot — a Rails (backend) + Vue 3 (frontend) open-source customer-engagement / omnichannel support platform. An **Account** is the tenant; an **Inbox** is a communication channel (web widget, email, WhatsApp, SMS, etc.); **Conversations** and **Contacts** flow through inboxes.

**Layout (Rails-conventional, plus an Enterprise overlay):**
- `app/models/` — domain models; `app/controllers/`, `app/policies/`, `app/builders/`, `app/finders/`, `app/services/`, `app/jobs/`, `app/listeners/`, `app/views/` (jbuilder).
- `lib/` — integrations (`lib/integrations/...`), seeders, rake tasks, Redis keys, event types.
- `enterprise/` — a paid-tier overlay that **prepends/includes modules into OSS classes** via `prepend_mod_with` / `include_mod_with` (e.g. `Inbox.prepend_mod_with('Inbox')` at `app/models/inbox.rb:269-271`). Always check `enterprise/` for a mirror when touching core.
- `app/javascript/` — Vue 3 frontend; `spec/` — RSpec; `db/` — schema/migrations.

**The model being reworked.** `Inbox` lives at **`app/models/inbox.rb`** (`class Inbox` at line 41). An external messaging channel hangs off it via a **polymorphic `belongs_to :channel` (`app/models/inbox.rb:60`)** whose concrete types are in `app/models/channel/` (api, email, web_widget, whatsapp, facebook_page, instagram, sms, telegram, line, tiktok, twilio_sms, twitter_profile). Conversations/contacts attach via `has_many :conversations` (`:68`), `has_many :contact_inboxes`/`:contacts` (`:63-64`).

**Shared pieces it's built on (concerns, all in `app/models/concerns/`):** `Reportable` (`reportable.rb`), `Avatarable` (`avatarable.rb`), `OutOfOffisable` (`out_of_offisable.rb`), `AccountCacheRevalidator` (`account_cache_revalidator.rb`), `InboxAgentAvailability` (`inbox_agent_availability.rb`) — included at `app/models/inbox.rb:43-47`. The reverse side of the channel link is `Channelable` (`app/models/concerns/channelable.rb`), included by every `Channel::*`.

**Enterprise overlays of Inbox:** `enterprise/app/models/enterprise/inbox.rb`, `enterprise/app/models/enterprise/concerns/inbox.rb`, `enterprise/app/models/enterprise/audit/inbox.rb`, `enterprise/app/models/enterprise/inbox_agent_availability.rb`.

## Step 2 — The Inbox contract and what it's built on

**Model:** `app/models/inbox.rb` (`class Inbox`, line 41).

**Concerns it pulls in (shared behavior):**
- `Reportable` — `app/models/inbox.rb:43` → `app/models/concerns/reportable.rb:7` adds `has_many :reporting_events, dependent: :destroy` (note: **inline `:destroy`, not async**).
- `Avatarable` — `:44` → `app/models/concerns/avatarable.rb:10` `has_one_attached :avatar`, `:12` `after_save :fetch_avatar_from_gravatar`.

- `AccountCacheRevalidator` — `:46` → `app/models/concerns/account_cache_revalidator.rb:5` `after_commit :update_account_cache, on: [:create,:update,:destroy]`.
- `InboxAgentAvailability` — `:47` → `app/models/concerns/inbox_agent_availability.rb` `available_agents`, `member_ids_with_assignment_capacity`, `fetch_online_agent_ids`.

**Associations other records hang off it** (`app/models/inbox.rb`):
- `belongs_to :channel, polymorphic: true, dependent: :destroy` (`:60`) — the channel is destroyed **inline** with the inbox.
- `belongs_to :account` (`:57`), `belongs_to :portal, optional: true` (`:58`).

**Key takeaway:** the destroy contract is **mixed** — `:channel`, `:inbox_assignment_policy`, `:reporting_events` (via Reportable), `:captain_inbox`, `:inbox_capacity_limits` destroy **inline**; everything else is `destroy_async`. Changing any `dependent:` changes which records are torn down synchronously vs. on a background queue.

---

## Step 3 — Teardown trace (inbox + attached data)

Order of execution when an inbox is destroyed:

1. **Trigger (request layer):** `Api::V1::Accounts::InboxesController#destroy` (`app/controllers/api/v1/accounts/inboxes_controller.rb:77-80`) → enqueues `DeleteObjectJob.perform_later(@inbox, Current.user, request.ip)`. (Account-level teardown enters the same job: `app/controllers/super_admin/accounts_controller.rb:60-63` → `DeleteObjectJob.perform_later(account)`.)

2. **Job — pre-purge heavy associations (inline, batched):** `DeleteObjectJob#perform` (`app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb`) calls `purge_heavy_associations` **before** `object.destroy!`. For an `Inbox`, `heavy_associations` (`:18-22`) = `%i[conversations contact_inboxes reporting_events]`; `batch_destroy` (`:36-40`) walks them in batches of 5,000 calling `destroy!` on each record **synchronously**, to avoid `destroy_async` fan-out timeouts/races. (For an `Account` it's `conversations contacts inboxes reporting_events`; the Enterprise overlay `enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/delete_object_job.rb` also adds `SlaPolicy => [:applied_slas]`.)

3. **`object.destroy!`** (`app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb`, after purge) fires the model `dependent:` chain on `app/models/inbox.rb`:
   - **Inline (`:destroy`):** `channel` (`:60`, the `Channel::*` row + its own `has_one :inbox dependent: :destroy_async`), `inbox_assignment_policy` (`:71`), `reporting_events` (Reportable, `reportable.rb:7`), and Enterprise `captain_inbox` + `inbox_capacity_limits`.
   - **Async (`:destroy_async`, enqueues `ActiveRecord::DestroyAssociationAsyncJob`):** `campaigns`, `contact_inboxes` (→ each `has_many :conversations dependent: :destroy_async`, `app/models/contact_inbox.rb:34`), `inbox_members`, `conversations` (→ `messages`, `mentions`, `csat_survey_response`, `conversation_participants`, `notifications`, `reporting_events` — `app/models/conversation.rb:109-115`), `messages`, `webhooks`, `hooks`, `agent_bot_inbox`, `working_hours` (OutOfOffisable), plus Enterprise `calls`.
   - Note conversations/contact_inboxes are *already* purged inline in step 2, so the async pass typically finds them gone — the async declaration is the fallback path for the non-batched (direct `inbox.destroy!`) route.

4. **`after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents`** (`app/models/inbox.rb:80,260`) → `AutoAssignment::InboxRoundRobinService#clear_queue` (`app/services/auto_assignment/inbox_round_robin_service.rb:5-7`) deletes the inbox's Redis round-robin key. `after_commit :update_account_cache` (AccountCacheRevalidator) also runs.

5. **Post-deletion (Enterprise only):** `DeleteObjectJob#process_post_deletion_tasks` → `enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/delete_object_job.rb#create_audit_entry` writes an `Enterprise::AuditLog` (`action: 'destroy'`) for `Inbox`/`Conversation`/`SlaPolicy` when a `user` is present.

**Async vs inline summary:** *Inline* = channel, inbox_assignment_policy, reporting_events, (EE) captain_inbox, inbox_capacity_limits, **and the batched purge of conversations/contact_inboxes/reporting_events in the job**. *Async* = campaigns, inbox_members, agent_bot_inbox, webhooks, hooks, working_hours, (EE) calls, and the declarative conversation/contact_inbox/message paths.

## Step 5 — How inbox availability & identity are guarded in background work

**Assignment (per-inbox bulk):**
- `AutoAssignment::PeriodicAssignmentJob#perform` (`app/jobs/auto_assignment/periodic_assignment_job.rb:4-18`) — re-loads inboxes fresh each run via `account.inboxes.joins(:assignment_policy).find_in_batches` (`:9`); double-guards with `account.feature_enabled?('assignment_v2')` (`:7`) and `inbox.auto_assignment_v2_enabled?` (`:11`, → `app/models/inbox.rb:206`). It passes only `inbox.id` forward (`:13`), never a stale object.
- `AutoAssignment::AssignmentJob.enqueue_for_inbox` (`app/jobs/auto_assignment/assignment_job.rb:9-23`) — Redis in-flight lock keyed by `inbox_id` (`AUTO_ASSIGNMENT_IN_FLIGHT_KEY`) so at most one job per inbox; token-based compare-and-delete release (`:44-49`).
- `AutoAssignment::AssignmentJob#perform` (`:25-37`) — **identity guard:** `inbox = Inbox.find_by(id: inbox_id); return unless inbox` (`:26-27`) — re-fetches by id and bails if the inbox was destroyed between enqueue and run.
- `AutoAssignment::AssignmentService` (`app/services/auto_assignment/assignment_service.rb`) — `return 0 unless inbox.auto_assignment_v2_enabled?` (`:5`), `return 0 unless inbox.enable_auto_assignment?` (`:6`), `return 0 if inbox.available_agents.empty?` (`:10`); row-locks conversations (`:94`) and `conversation.reload` (`:79`).
- Round-robin: `AutoAssignment::InboxRoundRobinService#validate_queue?` (`inbox_round_robin_service.rb:51-53`) reconciles the Redis queue against `inbox.inbox_members` before serving, `reset_queue` (`:23`) on mismatch; `AgentAssignmentService` re-derives the service from `conversation.inbox` (`agent_assignment_service.rb:32`).

**Fetching (IMAP/email):**
- `Inboxes::FetchImapEmailInboxesJob#should_fetch_emails?` (`app/jobs/inboxes/fetch_imap_email_inboxes_job.rb:14-22`) — guards `inbox.account.suspended?`, `inbox.channel.imap_enabled`, `inbox.channel.reauthorization_required?`, plus cloud/plan checks; iterates a fresh `Inbox.where(channel_type:'Channel::Email').find_each` (`:6-7`).
- `Inboxes::FetchImapEmailsJob` (`fetch_imap_emails_job.rb`) — operates on the **channel**, re-checks `should_fetch_email?` = `imap_enabled? && !reauthorization_required?` (`:27-29`); `MutexApplicationJob` lock keyed by `channel.inbox.id` (`:9-13`); `channel.authorization_error!` on OAuth failure (`:45`, drives Reauthorizable threshold).

**Periodic maintenance / orphans:**
- `Internal::RemoveStaleContactInboxesService` (`remove_stale_contact_inboxes_service.rb`) — env-flag gated (`:24-28`); selects via `ContactInbox.stale_without_conversations` (`contact_inbox.rb:37`) and batched `delete_all` (`:14-19`).
- `Internal::RemoveOrphanConversationsService` (`remove_orphan_conversations_service.rb:26-32`) — **the explicit teardown-safety net:** `left_outer_joins(:contact, :inbox)` then `where(inboxes: { id: nil })` finds conversations whose inbox row is gone (a direct consequence of async teardown leaving orphans) and `destroy_all`s them; invoked from `lib/tasks/ops/cleanup_orphan_conversations.rake`.

**Pattern:** background code never trusts a passed-in inbox object — it either passes `inbox_id` and re-`find`s (`return unless inbox`), re-queries from the account/`Inbox` scope, or guards on `account.suspended?` / feature flags / `reauthorization_required?` before acting.

## Step 6 — Blast radius of the contract change (ranked by risk)

The change touches **teardown** and **a couple of associations'** behavior. Risk ranking:

**🔴 Critical — most likely to break / page someone:**
1. `app/jobs/delete_object_job.rb` (+ `enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/delete_object_job.rb`) — the `heavy_associations` map (`:18-22`) hard-codes `Inbox => [conversations, contact_inboxes, reporting_events]`. **If you rename/repurpose any of those associations or change destroy semantics, the batched purge silently no-ops** (it guards `next unless object.respond_to?(assoc)`), leaving heavy data behind → orphan storms. Highest blast radius.
2. The `dependent:` declarations on `app/models/inbox.rb:60-76` — changing inline↔async on `channel` (`:60`), `inbox_assignment_policy` (`:71`), `reporting_events` (Reportable `:7`), or any async member alters what's torn down synchronously. Async fan-out (`ActiveRecord::DestroyAssociationAsyncJob`) is what the job exists to avoid; regressing it reintroduces destroy timeouts.
3. `Internal::RemoveOrphanConversationsService` / `cleanup_orphan_conversations.rake` (`:26-32`) — depends on `conversations.belongs_to :inbox` being nullable-after-teardown. If teardown order or FK behavior changes, this query's `inboxes: { id: nil }` semantics shift.
4. Enterprise teardown overlay: `enterprise/app/models/enterprise/concerns/inbox.rb` (`captain_inbox dependent: :destroy`, `inbox_capacity_limits dependent: :destroy`, `calls dependent: :destroy_async`) and `enterprise/app/jobs/enterprise/delete_object_job.rb` audit log — must be re-verified in lockstep; OSS-only testing will miss these.

**🟠 High — strong coupling to teardown/associations:**
5. `app/models/concerns/channelable.rb:6` — the reverse `has_one :inbox, dependent: :destroy_async, touch: true`. Channel-side teardown (account destroy hits `Channel::*` directly via `account.rb:78-100`) and inbox-side `belongs_to :channel dependent: :destroy` form a **two-way destroy** — a change here risks destroy loops or double-destroy.
6. `app/models/account.rb:83` `has_many :inboxes, dependent: :destroy_async` + the account path through the same `DeleteObjectJob` (`heavy_associations[Account]` includes `inboxes`). Account teardown is the larger-scale exercise of the same contract.
7. `app/models/contact_inbox.rb:34` (`has_many :conversations, dependent: :destroy_async`) and `:37` stale scope — sit between inbox and conversations in the destroy chain.
8. `after_destroy :delete_round_robin_agents` (`inbox.rb:80,260`) + `inbox_member.rb:25-26` round-robin Redis cleanup — Redis side-effects on destroy.

**🟡 Medium — read/validate the inbox, break only if identity/availability assumptions change:**
9. Assignment pipeline: `assignment_job.rb:26-27` (`find_by` guard), `assignment_service.rb`, `periodic_assignment_job.rb`, round-robin services — already defensively guarded (Step 5) but exercise the inbox heavily.
10. IMAP fetch jobs (`fetch_imap_email*.rb`) — operate on `channel`/`channel.inbox`; if the channel↔inbox link timing changes during teardown, `channel.inbox.id` references (`:9,15,18`) could NPE.
11. `Integrations::Hook` (`hook.rb:40`, `attr_readonly :inbox_id`), `webhook.rb:23` (optional), `reporting_event.rb:37` (optional), `working_hour.rb:24`, `campaign.rb:45` — FK holders; verify nullify-vs-destroy expectations.

**🟢 Lower — mostly read-path, validate-after:**
12. Reports builders (`app/builders/v2/reports/*`), finders (`conversation_finder.rb`), filter/search services, provider send/incoming services — consume inbox attributes/predicates; break only if the public method surface (Step 2) changes, not the teardown.

**Must re-verify after the change:** the full `DeleteObjectJob` path (inbox destroy + account destroy) in both OSS and Enterprise; no orphaned `conversations`/`contact_inboxes`/`messages`/`reporting_events`/`calls`/`applied_slas`; Redis cleanup (round-robin key, in-flight key, IMAP mutex); Enterprise audit-log creation; assignment job's `return unless inbox` guard still fires; orphan-cleanup service query still matches.
